definition: An area in the price chart where there was NO trading volume — price jumped through this zone without any order interaction. Unlike FVGs (which are price gaps), liquidity voids are VOLUME gaps.
depends_on: volume_profile (volume data reveals where voids exist)
feeds_into: trade entry (voids act as magnets — price tends to return to fill them)
related_to: fair_value_gaps (FVG = price gap, liquidity_void = volume gap — related but different)
vs_FVG: FVG: gap between candle 1 high and candle 3 low (pure price pattern). Liquidity void: area where volume profile shows zero or near-zero traded volume. An FVG CAN be a liquidity void, but not all FVGs are voids, and voids can exist without FVG pattern.
how_to_use: Price fills liquidity voids before continuing. If a void exists between entry and TP, expect price to pause there. If a void exists behind price, expect price to retrace to fill it.
